title: 清洁女工之死
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\209-清洁女工之死-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Superintendent Spence informs Hercule Poirot that the investigation into the death of Mrs. McKinty is being closed because Spence has been reassigned to a corruption case in Scotland. Although Spence admits he has found no evidence to suggest anyone other than the lodger, James Bentley, killed her, he asks Poirot to take over the case. Spence believes the murder was not a suicide and hopes Poirot's methods might uncover what he missed. Motivated by boredom and a desire to help Spence find peace of mind, Poirot accepts the challenge.

Spence provides specific details regarding the victim, Mrs. McKinty, a sixty-four-year-old widow living in Broadhurst. She had £200 in a bank account and an additional £30 hidden under a loose floorboard in her bedroom, a fact known to both her niece and James Bentley. While the niece and her husband, a painter, are considered suspects, they appear to have no motive or need for the money.

James Bentley, a well-educated but struggling man, had been renting a room from Mrs. McKinty and was nearly out of money due to unpaid rent. On the night of the murder, November 22nd, Bentley claimed to have been out for a walk from 7:15 p.m. to 9:00 p.m. Mrs. McKinty died between 8:30 and 9:00 p.m. after being struck on the back of the head with a sharp object, possibly a butcher's knife. Although the windows were locked from the inside and there were no signs of forced entry, the £30 was missing from beneath the lifted floorboard. The body was discovered the following morning by Mrs. Elliott after Bentley and a baker knocked on the door.
